summary: Regulatory T cells (Treg) such as CD4(+ )CD25 (+ FOXP3 + cells suppress immune responses rather than enhancing them; thus, they serve as **negative**, not **positive**, regulators of immunity due to mechanisms including secretion of anti-inflammatory cytokines and modulation of antigen-presenting cell functions. This role is critical for maintaining self-tolerance and preventing excessive inflammatory reactions.

This role is critical for maintaining self-tolerance and preventing excessive inflammatory reactions.. Answer choice: B
